sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed after clicking on the purple block. Let me check what changed by comparing the before and after frames.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking the purple block
# Compare previous_frame to current_frame
if last_transition and last_transition.before_frame:
    before_seg = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
    after_seg = current_frame.segmentation
    
    before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before_seg['nodes']}
    after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after_seg['nodes']}
    
    print("Before nodes count:", len(before_nodes))
    print("After nodes count:", len(after_nodes))
    
    # Check for changes in each node
    for nid in after_nodes:
        if nid in before_nodes:
            bn = before_nodes[nid]
            an = after_nodes[nid]
            if bn['hash'] != an['hash'] or bn['boundary'] != an['boundary'] or bn['pixels'] != an['pixels']:
                print(f"Node {nid} CHANGED: color {bn['color']}->{an['color']}, pixels {bn['pixels']}->{an['pixels']}")
                print(f"  boundary before: {bn['boundary']}")
                print(f"  boundary after: {an['boundary']}")
        else:
            print(f"Node {nid} NEW: color={after_nodes[nid]['color']}, pixels={after_nodes[nid]['pixels']}")
    
    for nid in before_nodes:
        if nid not in after_nodes:
            print(f"Node {nid} REMOVED: color={before_nodes[nid]['color']}")
else:
    print("No transition to compare")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The only changes are in the bottom border area (nodes 10 and 14) - the black background expanded slightly and the charcoal border shrank. This seems like a timer/progress bar change, not a gameplay change. The actual puzzle objects didn't change.
So clicking on the purple block didn't do anything meaningful. Let me try a different approach - maybe I need to use arrow keys or SPACE, or maybe I need to click on something else.
Let me look more carefully at the board. The top-right panel has small indicator squares - maybe those are interactive. Let me also try using arrow keys to see if there's a cursor that moves around.
